# UC-18.4.11 · NDFC Flow Telemetry Drop and Export Health

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Performance, Availability, Data Quality &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch whether flow samples from your data-center switches still reach the analytics collectors, and we raise a hand when drops or stalled exports would leave east-west traffic pictures incomplete for your team.*

---

## Description

Detects collector-side drops, stalled NetFlow/IPFIX export rates, and controller-plane pauses across Cisco NDFC-managed LAN fabrics by correlating cisco:ndfc:flow_telemetry, cisco:ndfc:collector_health, cisco:ndfc:fabric_status, cisco:ndfc:audit, and cisco:ndfc:syslog streams keyed on fabric_name and collector_id. Surfaces buckets where flow_count momentum diverges from historical medians, drop_count accumulates, or REST-driven config-preview and PMN telemetry status counters disagree with live exporter claims before east-west utilization and security hunting lose five-tuple fidelity.

## Value

Capacity planners and incident leads keep trustworthy east-west demand curves and forensic coverage because Splunk preserves independent evidence that Nexus Dashboard flow pipelines remained healthy—avoiding silent blind spots during congestion hunts, budget cycles, and customer-impacting events where missing flow tuples would otherwise masquerade as normal traffic.

## Implementation

(1) Land five cisco:ndfc:* sourcetypes on index=cisco_dc with Splunkbase 1546 pollers hitting /pmn/telemetry/status and per-fabric config-preview paths; normalize fabric_name and collector_id fields. (2) Install Splunkbase 7777 for packaged fabric tokens; optionally deploy 4022/4856/1810 when hybrid APIC or NSX context explains timing. (3) Schedule the SPL alert with maintenance_allow_list suppression and tune eps_floor against site baselines.

## Evidence

Saved search ndfc_flow_telemetry_pipeline_health, Dashboard panel DC_NDFC_FlowTelemetry_Pipeline_18_4_11, and weekly CSV exports archived to network_operations index with 13-month retention.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Lab fabric ingests cisco:ndfc:flow_telemetry with med_eps near 800 eps, then collector_health injects drop_count>0 and export_rate falls to 50 eps while cisco:ndfc:audit shows simultaneous policy publish; SPL returns sev1_dual_failure or sev2_export_stall_with_control_plane with non-zero ctl_plane_events.

### Negative scenario

Lookup maintenance_allow_list marks the same fabric_key during an approved ND upgrade; stalled clears after two buckets and operator acknowledges drill—alert macro suppresses email per valid_until window.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Nexus Dashboard Fabric Controller (NDFC) 12.4.x must register LAN fabrics whose NX-OS leaves export NetFlow/IPFIX toward Nexus Dashboard collectors; confirm licensing before expecting flow_count fields in Splunk.
- Install cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)) so dashboard macros align with **cisco:ndfc:flow_telemetry**, **cisco:ndfc:collector_health**, **cisco:ndfc:fabric_status**, **cisco:ndfc:audit**, and **cisco:ndfc:syslog** sourcetypes.
- Deploy Splunk REST API Modular Input ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) with bearer tokens for **GET /appcenter/cisco/ndfc/api/v1/pmn/telemetry/status** and **GET /appcenter/cisco/ndfc/api/v1/lan-fabric/rest/control/fabrics/{fabric-id}/config-preview**; use 60–180s polls unless Cisco TAC advises tighter cadences during incidents.
- Optional cross-domain joins: Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)) for brownfield fabric_name alignment; Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) plus Splunk Add-on for VMware ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) when NSX maintenance correlates in time but root cause is outside NDFC.
- Document read-only LAN REST RBAC for automation accounts; store credentials in Splunk secret stanzas—not macros queried by casual analysts.
- Land data in **index=cisco_dc** with retention covering monthly capacity reviews; budget for five sourcetype streams per fabric plus syslog fan-out.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) **cisco:ndfc:flow_telemetry** — Ingest exporter summaries with **flow_count**, **export_rate**, and drop counters; normalize **fabric_name** and **collector_id** at ingest.
(2) **cisco:ndfc:collector_health** — Capture queue and disk pressure mapped to **drop_count** and **export_rate**; keep UTC timestamps aligned with switch observation windows reported by IPFIX.
(3) **cisco:ndfc:fabric_status** — Mirror LAN inventory reachability so REST health changes still list exporters expected in flow pipelines.
(4) **cisco:ndfc:audit** — Record operator changes (policy publish, controller reloads, config-preview commits) that can pause telemetry without physical failures.
(5) **cisco:ndfc:syslog** — Forward facility local6/local7 lines mentioning telemetry, FLOW, IPFIX, or gRPC errors for faster triage than waiting on SNMP polling cycles.

### Step 2 — Create the search and alert
#### Understanding this SPL: fifteen-minute buckets aggregate flow and collector lanes per **fabric_key** and **collector_key** so drops and rate collapse appear together. **med_eps** fabric medians replace static EPS guesses; tune the 0.35 multiplier on **eps_floor** when oversampling increases legitimate throughput.

```spl
index=cisco_dc (sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:flow_telemetry" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:collector_health" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:fabric_status" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:audit" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:syslog")
earliest=-6h@m
| eval fabric_key=trim(lower(coalesce(fabric_name,fabricName,fabric,nd_fabric,fabric_id,siteFabric)))
| eval collector_key=trim(lower(coalesce(collector_id,collectorId,collector_uuid,collectorGUID,exporter_id,exporterId,telemetry_exporter)))
| eval flow_count=tonumber(coalesce(flow_count,flow_records,flows_observed,active_flows,flows_total,record_count))
| eval drop_count=tonumber(coalesce(drop_count,dropped_flows,drops,export_drops,queue_drops,telemetry_drops,buffer_drops))
| eval export_rate=tonumber(coalesce(export_rate,export_rate_eps,flows_per_second,export_eps,eps,telemetry_eps,flows_eps))
| eval st=lower(sourcetype)
| eval lane=case(
    match(st,"flow_telemetry"),"flow",
    match(st,"collector_health"),"collector",
    match(st,"fabric_status"),"fabric_rest",
    match(st,"ndfc:audit"),"audit",
    match(st,"ndfc:syslog"),"syslog",
    1=1,"other")
| eval ctl_plane=if(match(st,"ndfc:audit") OR match(st,"ndfc:syslog"),1,0)
| where isnotnull(fabric_key) AND fabric_key!="" AND fabric_key!="unknown"
| bin _time span=15m
| stats
    sum(eval(if(lane="flow",coalesce(flow_count,0),null))) AS sum_flow_count,
    sum(eval(if(lane IN ("flow","collector"),coalesce(drop_count,0),null))) AS sum_drop_count,
    avg(eval(if(lane IN ("flow","collector"),export_rate,null))) AS avg_export_rate,
    latest(eval(if(lane="collector",export_rate,null))) AS last_coll_eps,
    sum(ctl_plane) AS ctl_plane_events,
    values(lane) AS lanes_seen
  BY _time fabric_key collector_key
| eventstats median(avg_export_rate) AS med_eps BY fabric_key
| eval eps_floor=if(isnotnull(med_eps) AND med_eps>0, round(0.35*med_eps,4), null())
| eval stalled=if(isnotnull(avg_export_rate) AND isnotnull(eps_floor) AND avg_export_rate<eps_floor AND med_eps>50,1,0)
| eval lossy=if(sum_drop_count>0,1,0)
| eval ctl_hit=if(ctl_plane_events>0,1,0)
| where lossy=1 OR stalled=1 OR (last_coll_eps<1 AND med_eps>25)
| eval severity=case(
    lossy=1 AND stalled=1 AND sum_drop_count>50000,"sev1_dual_failure",
    stalled=1 AND ctl_hit=1,"sev2_export_stall_with_control_plane",
    lossy=1,"sev3_collector_loss",
    stalled=1,"sev3_export_slowdown",
    1=1,"sev4_watch")
| eval narrative=case(
    severity=="sev1_dual_failure","Exporter throughput collapsed while drops accumulated—treat as priority fabric pipeline incident.",
    severity=="sev2_export_stall_with_control_plane","Telemetry plane quiet while controller audit or syslog noise rose—validate REST tokens, policy publish, or ND worker recycling.",
    severity=="sev3_collector_loss","Collector drops exceed baseline—check HEC pacing, disk IO, and leaf CPU for sampler pressure.",
    severity=="sev3_export_slowdown","Flow records arriving far below median rate—compare recent IPFIX template or interface changes.",
    1=1,"Review adjacent buckets for trend before paging.")
| table _time fabric_key collector_key lanes_seen sum_flow_count sum_drop_count avg_export_rate med_eps last_coll_eps stalled lossy ctl_hit severity narrative
| sort -sum_drop_count stalled -_time
| head 250
```

Pipeline walkthrough — Stage 1 scopes **index=cisco_dc** and five **cisco:ndfc:** sourcetypes across six hours. Stage 2 coalesces renames Cisco introduces in JSON payloads. Stage 3 applies `bin` to `_time` with a 15m span to smooth burst noise. Stage 4 **stats** summarizes while retaining **lanes_seen** for drilldowns. Stage 5 **eventstats** derives **med_eps** per fabric for relative slowdown detection. Stage 6 labels **severity** strings tied to alert routing.

### Step 3 — Validate
(1) Compare with the NDFC Flow Telemetry dashboard and reconcile Splunk **sum_flow_count** totals for the same fabric hour; investigate FIELDALIAS drift or forwarder clock skew when variance exceeds operator expectations.
(2) Compare **GET /pmn/telemetry/status** JSON with **cisco:ndfc:collector_health** events at the same minute—status fields should track **export_rate** within sampling tolerance.
(3) Run **timechart span=15m** counts by sourcetype; a flat **cisco:ndfc:flow_telemetry** line while **cisco:ndfc:audit** spikes suggests controller-side pauses rather than WAN loss.
(4) After automation runs, verify **config-preview** still lists exporters that **show run netflow** on leaves; missing entries indicate inventory gaps upstream of Splunk.
(5) Validate **collector_id** cardinality against Nexus Dashboard inventory exports; unknown GUIDs often follow VIP failovers or DNS lag on collector clusters.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
(1) Capacity dashboard: combine area charts of **sum_flow_count** with overlays of **sum_drop_count** per **fabric_key**.
(2) Operator table: sort by **severity**, pass **fabric_key** and **collector_key** as drilldown tokens into saved searches that replay raw sourcetypes.
(3) Weekly CSV summaries with **narrative** text attached to CAB bridges for audit-friendly history.
(4) RBAC: scope roles so multi-tenant operators cannot search unrelated fabrics in **index=cisco_dc**.
(5) For very large fleets, schedule heavy searches off-peak or write to summaries to protect search head budgets.

### Step 5 — Visualization, alerting, and troubleshooting
Visualization — Timechart **avg_export_rate** per **collector_key**, single value for latest **sum_drop_count**, optional heatmap of **stalled** flags pivoting **fabric_key** rows.

Alerting — **sev1_dual_failure**: page fabric anchor and telemetry SME; throttle 30 minutes per **fabric_key**. **sev2_export_stall_with_control_plane**: notify automation with **ctl_plane_events** totals; require acknowledgement before suppression. **sev3** lanes: email digest when **maintenance_allow_list** lookup misses.

Troubleshooting — No cisco:ndfc:flow_telemetry events usually means exporter drift or DNS issues toward collectors; confirm NX-OS feature netflow/ipfix, exporter destination reachability, and sampler maps in the correct VRF. REST API error 401/403 on Splunkbase **1546** modular inputs: rotate ND bearer tokens and revalidate IdP group claims. HTTP 429 responses: increase poll interval or shard fabrics across inputs. Null **export_rate** all day: collector maintenance window or revoked HEC token—check **splunkd.log** on forwarders running REST collectors. High **drop_count** with calm CPU: inspect MTU or policer issues plus collector storage latency. Stale **fabric_name**: refresh **config-preview** pulls and update **ndfc_fabric_alias** lookup tables feeding dashboards.

Field hygiene appendix — Version-controlled props should map **fabricName**, **nd_fabric**, **collectorGuid**, **flowsExported**, **flowsPerSecond**, **dropCount**, **telemetryHealth**, **lastSyncEpoch**, **templateId**, **observationPointId**, **exporterIPv4**, **samplerMode**, **activeTimeWindow**, **http_status**, **poll_latency_ms**, **grpc_backpressure**, **he_queue_depth**, **nd_worker_partition**, **vip_role**, **lag_suffix** into the canonical names this search expects; record the pull request identifier in your CMDB bridge whenever Cisco adjusts JSON during NDFC maintenance bundles.

## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_dc (sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:flow_telemetry" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:collector_health" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:fabric_status" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:audit" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:syslog")
earliest=-6h@m
| eval fabric_key=trim(lower(coalesce(fabric_name,fabricName,fabric,nd_fabric,fabric_id,siteFabric)))
| eval collector_key=trim(lower(coalesce(collector_id,collectorId,collector_uuid,collectorGUID,exporter_id,exporterId,telemetry_exporter)))
| eval flow_count=tonumber(coalesce(flow_count,flow_records,flows_observed,active_flows,flows_total,record_count))
| eval drop_count=tonumber(coalesce(drop_count,dropped_flows,drops,export_drops,queue_drops,telemetry_drops,buffer_drops))
| eval export_rate=tonumber(coalesce(export_rate,export_rate_eps,flows_per_second,export_eps,eps,telemetry_eps,flows_eps))
| eval st=lower(sourcetype)
| eval lane=case(
    match(st,"flow_telemetry"),"flow",
    match(st,"collector_health"),"collector",
    match(st,"fabric_status"),"fabric_rest",
    match(st,"ndfc:audit"),"audit",
    match(st,"ndfc:syslog"),"syslog",
    1=1,"other")
| eval ctl_plane=if(match(st,"ndfc:audit") OR match(st,"ndfc:syslog"),1,0)
| where isnotnull(fabric_key) AND fabric_key!="" AND fabric_key!="unknown"
| bin _time span=15m
| stats
    sum(eval(if(lane="flow",coalesce(flow_count,0),null))) AS sum_flow_count,
    sum(eval(if(lane IN ("flow","collector"),coalesce(drop_count,0),null))) AS sum_drop_count,
    avg(eval(if(lane IN ("flow","collector"),export_rate,null))) AS avg_export_rate,
    latest(eval(if(lane="collector",export_rate,null))) AS last_coll_eps,
    sum(ctl_plane) AS ctl_plane_events,
    values(lane) AS lanes_seen
  BY _time fabric_key collector_key
| eventstats median(avg_export_rate) AS med_eps BY fabric_key
| eval eps_floor=if(isnotnull(med_eps) AND med_eps>0, round(0.35*med_eps,4), null())
| eval stalled=if(isnotnull(avg_export_rate) AND isnotnull(eps_floor) AND avg_export_rate<eps_floor AND med_eps>50,1,0)
| eval lossy=if(sum_drop_count>0,1,0)
| eval ctl_hit=if(ctl_plane_events>0,1,0)
| where lossy=1 OR stalled=1 OR (last_coll_eps<1 AND med_eps>25)
| eval severity=case(
    lossy=1 AND stalled=1 AND sum_drop_count>50000,"sev1_dual_failure",
    stalled=1 AND ctl_hit=1,"sev2_export_stall_with_control_plane",
    lossy=1,"sev3_collector_loss",
    stalled=1,"sev3_export_slowdown",
    1=1,"sev4_watch")
| eval narrative=case(
    severity=="sev1_dual_failure","Exporter throughput collapsed while drops accumulated—treat as priority fabric pipeline incident.",
    severity=="sev2_export_stall_with_control_plane","Telemetry plane quiet while controller audit or syslog noise rose—validate REST tokens, policy publish, or ND worker recycling.",
    severity=="sev3_collector_loss","Collector drops exceed baseline—check HEC pacing, disk IO, and leaf CPU for sampler pressure.",
    severity=="sev3_export_slowdown","Flow records arriving far below median rate—compare recent IPFIX template or interface changes.",
    1=1,"Review adjacent buckets for trend before paging.")
| table _time fabric_key collector_key lanes_seen sum_flow_count sum_drop_count avg_export_rate med_eps last_coll_eps stalled lossy ctl_hit severity narrative
| sort -sum_drop_count stalled -_time
| head 250
```

## Visualization

(1) Timechart of avg_export_rate by collector_key with reference band from med_eps. (2) Single value + sparkline for sum_drop_count. (3) Table of fabric_key, collector_key, severity, narrative, ctl_plane_events for operators. (4) Heatmap of stalled flag by fabric_key across buckets for management standups. (5) Alert workflow panel wiring sev1_dual_failure to pagerDuty, sev2_export_stall_with_control_plane to automation Slack, sev3 lanes to email digest via Splunk alert actions.

## Known False Positives

**warm_restart_window** — Rolling recycling of Nexus Dashboard workers that host PMN telemetry can zero out export_rate readings for several minutes while switches still sample flows; require stalled=1 across three consecutive 15m buckets before paging, and tie suppression to approved change tickets that cite ND cluster patching.

**sampler_headroom_change** — NetOps sometimes widen sampler intervals during congestion investigations; med_eps legitimately falls. Pair slowdown alerts with lossy=1 (non-zero drop_count) or a coincident spike in cisco:ndfc:audit before treating the fabric as broken.

**config_preview_partial** — LAN automation may omit border-leaf exporters inside config-preview JSON until inventory convergence finishes; low fabric_key cardinality alone is not a failure signal—compare against live NX-OS CLI and NDFC inventory tiles bundled with Splunkbase 7777 macros.

**aci_fabric_alias_collision** — Brownfield sites may reuse similar fabric labels in APIC and NDFC; Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI

- endpoint names can collide when coalescing fabric_name. Normalize via lookup tables ndfc_fabric_alias.csv and apic_fabric_bridge.csv before declaring cross-controller drift.

**nsx_edge_maintenance_overlap** — VMware NSX edge work ingested with Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX
- occasionally aligns timewise with flow telemetry dips even when NDFC is healthy; require CMDB tags or vCenter tasks from Splunk Add-on for VMware
- that reference the same maintenance scope before correlating.

**rest_poll_throttle_429** — Aggressive GET /pmn/telemetry/status polling after script redeploys can trip HTTP 429 counters echoed in cisco:ndfc:syslog without sustained collector drops; increase Splunk REST Modular Input
- intervals and clear false positives when drop_count stays zero.

**collector_drills_calendar** — Planned failover exercises rotate collector_key values to standby VIPs; median baselines reset for a full polling cycle. Use lookup collector_drill_suppress.csv keyed by drill_id with valid_until columns so sev3_export_slowdown notices stay informational during declared failover rehearsals.
